title: Entry Plug Ejection
wikitext:
[]: Entry Plug Ejection             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Wed Feb 09, 2011 5:12 pm Okay so this is something that i thought of while slightly inebriated, its something that came up when me and a couple of friends were talking about eva and i made a joke about entry plugs and then we got into a discussion and this is what i theorized...it may have holes but its still a theory the entry plugs dont eject because what does every mother want to do with their child when they are in danger. Hold them close and protect them its the mothers instinct. Now since the souls in the evas are the mothers then i thought that maybe this is just a mothers instict taking over and trying to keep the pilots as close to them as possible... now i realize that theres a big hole with the whole Rei and Mother Thing but this is just sumthing that i thought of and would like to see what other people think about this

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Wed Feb 09, 2011 5:27 pm I can't think of many instances where the plug wouldn't eject... Ep. 15 when EVA-01 is in a Sea of Dirac... Ep. 18 when Bardiel takes over EVA-03... Where else? Anyway, your theory would explain why EVA-01 and EVA-02 go berserk, while EVA-00 doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Wed Feb 09, 2011 5:30 pm DIDDY wrote: I can't think of many instances where the plug wouldn't eject... Ep. 15 when EVA-01 is in a Sea of Dirac... Ep. 18 when Bardiel takes over EVA-03... Where else? Anyway, your theory would explain why EVA-01 and EVA-02 go berserk, while EVA-00 doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Wed Feb 09, 2011 5:35 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: Ep. 19: they can't eject the plug when Shinji goes on his rampage

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 09, 2011 6:29 pm Eject or no eject, it all depends on the whim of the plot.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Feb 09, 2011 6:34 pm I think you're reading too much into this, the ejection system doesn't work because of Rule of Drama.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Wed Feb 09, 2011 7:50 pm Or, if you prefer, the law of narrative causality

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Wed Feb 09, 2011 7:59 pm Trajan wrote: I think you're reading too much into this, the ejection system doesn't work because of Rule of Drama.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Thu Feb 10, 2011 2:12 am The only single instance I can remember is the flashback into Eva-00's failed activation. Maybe also that time when Ireul attacked but those were simulation bodies so it doesn't(?) count.

[]:             Quote Post by apb123 » Thu Feb 10, 2011 2:43 pm I don't understand why the ejection system has to be directly tied into the Eva's systems. Why not install explosive bolts or something to blow the hatch off and then use the rockets in the Entry Plug to forcibly eject it?

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Thu Feb 10, 2011 4:52 pm apb123 wrote: Why not install explosive bolts or something to blow the hatch off and then use the rockets in the Entry Plug to forcibly eject it?

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Feb 11, 2011 6:28 am rhfxz1s wrote: I do chuckle at how many times the Evas reject the commands and signals sent by NERV. Now that I think about it, when do the Evas actually accept the signal to eject the entry plugs anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Feb 11, 2011 8:37 pm Face it, NERV just fails again and again, the plugs don't eject, EVAS go berserk or taken over by angels. S2 machines let everything in a wide radius disappear. It's an experimental technology and as such stuff like this is bound to happen.

Hold them close and protect them its the mothers instinct. Now since the souls in the evas are the mothers then i thought that maybe this is just a mothers instict taking over and trying to keep the pilots as close to them as possible... now i realize that theres a big hole with the whole Rei and Mother Thing but this is just sumthing that i thought of and would like to see what other people think about this

     Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Feb 11, 2011 8:37 pm Face it, NERV just fails again and again, the plugs don't eject, EVAS go berserk or taken over by angels. S2 machines let everything in a wide radius disappear. It's an experimental technology and as such stuff like this is bound to happen.
